up.method: Consumed as sole carbon source; residual glucose ~0 at steady state (YSI glucose analyser). Specific uptake rate (measured, Monte-Carlo-balanced) reported only in Additional data files 1/2, not in main text; no numeric value in article body.
Sole nitrogen source (NH4Cl 23.5 mM); enzymatic assay (Boehringer). Predicted C:N consumption ratio 9:1 in silico. Specific rate not numerically reported in main text.
Off-gas O2 measured by mass spectrometer (Prima White Box 600); used to compute OUR via gas balance. Numeric specific rate in Additional data files, not main text.
Sulfur source (Na2S2O3 0.38 mM); model-predicted preferred S source. Specific rate not numerically reported in main text.

sec.method: Excreted during growth on glucose (1H-NMR, JEOL 400 MHz). In silico: acetate secretion occurs when oxidation capacity limiting. Numeric specific rate in Additional data files, not main text.
Measured in culture supernatant by 1H-NMR; ethanol synthesized via alcohol dehydrogenase. No numeric specific rate in main text.
Off-gas CO2 measured by mass spectrometer; CO2 production rate computed via mass balance / Monte Carlo. Numeric value in Additional data files, not main text.

yields.note: Table 2 values are model-predicted (in silico) biomass yields on substrate (g biomass per Cmol substrate), not directly measured; glucose predicted as preferred carbon source. Maintenance requirement fixed at 2.81e-3 mol/g/h (from literature) in simulations.

growth_records.cite: Baart et al. Modeling Neisseria meningitidis metabolism: from genome to metabolic fluxes. Genome Biology (2007). DOI:10.1186/gb-2007-8-7-r136. [PMC2323225]
growth_records.snippet: operated in chemostat mode, with a working volume of 1.4 l. Temperature, pH, dissolved oxygen (DO) concentration, and stirrer speed were contolled at 37 C, 7.0, 30%, and 600 rpm... The growth rate was controlled at 0.04/h. Table 3 Medium co
growth_records.notes: Growth rate: chemostat at steady state, dilution rate D = specific growth rate mu = 0.04 h^-1 (explicitly 'growth rate was controlled at 0.04/h'). Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.04 = 17.33 h (calculated). Conditions verbatim: 37 C, pH 7.0, DO 30%, 600 rpm, gas flow 1.0 L/min, 1.4 L working volume, duplicate cultures. Medium = Table 3 (all mM unless stated) + trace-element solution (mM stock, added at 2.0 ml/l): CaCl2.2H2O 68, ZnSO4.7H2O 0.17, Na2MoO4.2H2O 0.08, MnCl2.4H2O 0.4, CoCl2.6H2O 0.04, CuSO4.5H2O 0.04. Substrates consumed: glucose (sole C, residual ~0), ammonium (sole N), O2, thiosulfate (S). Products excreted: acetate, ethanol, CO2. NO numeric specific uptake/secretion flux (qS, qO2, qCO2, qP) appears in the article body; the measured Monte-Carlo-balanced specific rates and full flux distribution are stated to reside in Additional data files 1 and 2 (supplementary), which are not part of the provided XML, so all rate fields left null rather than invented. Yields in Table 2 are in silico predictions (glucose 10.7 g/Cmol highest). Reported model result: 69 +/- 6% of pyruvate via Entner-Doudoroff cleavage.

growth_records.cite: Gerritzen, Matthias J H et al. High dissolved oxygen tension triggers outer membrane vesicle formation by Neisseria meningitidis. Microbial Cell Factories (2018). DOI:10.1186/s12934-018-1007-7. [PMC6171317]
growth_records.method: reported (steady-state chemostat mu = D = 0.03 h-1); doubling time calculated td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.03 = 23.1 h
growth_records.snippet: the biomass yield on substrate (Yxs) and the maintenance coefficient (ms) were 0.43 g_biomass.g_glucose-1 and 0.08 g_glucose.g_biomass-1 h-1, calculated using the maintenance model of Pirt... All chemostats showed depletion of the carbon so
growth_records.notes: Glucose-limited chemostat, lowest steady state at mu = D = 0.03 h-1 (accelerostat start point; carbon/glucose+glutamate limited). Study-measured Yxs = 0.43 g biomass/g glucose and maintenance ms = 0.08 g glucose/gDW/h (Pirt model). Derived specific glucose uptake qGlc = mu/Yxs + ms = 0.03/0.43 + 0.08 = 0.0698 + 0.08 = 0.150 g/gDW/h = 0.83 mmol/gDW/h (glucose MW 180.16). Growth also consumed glutamate (co-substrate) but no glutamate uptake rate given. Max specific growth rate on this medium reported as 0.5 h-1 (cited, ref 14). Chemostat T 35 C, pH 7.2, DO 30% air saturation. Medium is chemically defined but composition not stated here (cited to Baart et al., ref 27).

growth_records.method: reported (mu = D = 0.18 h-1, upper end of accelerostat/chemostat range); td = ln2/0.18 = 3.85 h
growth_records.snippet: the growth rate increases linearly with the CER up to a growth rate of 0.18 h-1. At higher dilution rates, a reduction in CER was observed and the experiment was stopped... Yxs 0.43 g_biomass.g_glucose-1 and ms 0.08
growth_records.notes: Glucose-limited chemostat/accelerostat upper steady state, mu = D = 0.18 h-1 (highest before CER drop). Same study-measured Yxs=0.43, ms=0.08. Derived qGlc = 0.18/0.43 + 0.08 = 0.4186 + 0.08 = 0.499 g/gDW/h = 2.77 mmol/gDW/h. Specific sOMV production was ~constant over mu 0.03-0.18 h-1, so growth rate is a minor OMV trigger. Three chemostat steady states were run at different D within 0.03-0.18 but their exact D values were not tabulated in text (shown only in Fig 1c); endpoints 0.03 and 0.18 captured as the two well-defined records. DO 30%, T 35 C, pH 7.2.
